channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: ASX closes higher as oil prices retreat on hopes of a US-Iran agreement
item.description: The Australian sharemarket finished higher on Monday after global oil prices dropped below US$100 a barrel amid growing optimism that the United States and Iran could reach an agreement to reopen the ...

item.title: Experts advise moderation in tuna consumption due to mercury concerns despite rising popularity
item.description: The article examines the growing popularity of tuna in the UK and the health considerations linked to its consumption, particularly concerns surrounding mercury exposure. According to figures released...

item.title: Assam tables Uniform Civil Code Bill proposing ban on polygamy and registration of live-in relationships
item.description: The Assam government has introduced a significant legislative proposal in the state assembly titled ‘The Uniform Civil Code, Assam, 2026 Bill’, aimed at reforming and consolidating personal laws gover...

item.title: Leopoldo Metlicovitz and the Development of Italian Art Nouveau Poster Design
item.description: Leopoldo Metlicovitz (1868–1944) was a highly influential Italian painter, illustrator, and stage designer who played a foundational role in the development of modern Italian poster art. Born in Tries...

item.title: Petrol and diesel prices rise again across India amid repeated fuel hikes and global crude volatility
item.description: Fuel prices across India have seen another sharp increase, with petrol in Delhi crossing the Rs 102 per litre mark after the fourth revision in just two weeks. On Monday, petrol prices in the national...

item.title: Sokoto ADC Stakeholders Endorse Atiku Abubakar for 2027 Presidential Election
item.description: The Sokoto State chapter of the African Democratic Congress (ADC) has publicly endorsed former Vice President Atiku Abubakar as its preferred presidential candidate for the 2027 general election. This...

item.title: Calgary woman alleges paralegal withheld $13,000 settlement amid concerns over lack of regulation in Alberta
item.description: A Calgary woman, Samantha Kirkpatrick, says she successfully secured a $13,000 settlement in a civil dispute against a contractor, but alleges that the paralegal she hired to represent her, John McDon...

item.title: House bill seeks free autism therapy, monthly allowance, and expanded support services for Filipino families
item.description: Isang panukalang batas ang inihain sa House of Representatives upang palawakin ang suporta ng gobyerno para sa mga persons with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) at kanilang mga pamilya. Inihain ni Deput...

item.title: How South Africa’s revised merger thresholds aim to streamline competition oversight
item.description: The article explains recent changes to South Africa’s merger control rules and why they matter for the broader economy and consumers. From 1 May 2026, the Competition Commission of South Africa update...

item.title: The career and life of Danish actress Lilian Ellis in European cinema and theatre (1920s–1940s)
item.description: The article presents a biographical overview of Danish actress, dancer, and performer Lilian Ellis (1907–1951), highlighting her artistic career across European cinema, theatre, and radio during the i...

item.title: 1940s American Gas Stations and the Rise of Full-Service Roadside Culture
item.description: The article explores the cultural and architectural significance of American gas stations during the 1940s, a period when they functioned as more than simple refueling stops. These stations embodied t...

item.title: Rare Behind-the-Scenes Photos Highlight the Making of Stanley Kubrick’s ‘Dr. Strangelove’
item.description: Vintage Everyday published a collection of 44 black-and-white behind-the-scenes photographs from the production of Stanley Kubrick’s 1964 film “Dr. Strangelove or: How I Learned to Stop Worrying and L...

item.title: Cruise ship cabin steward demonstrates how decorative towel animals are created for hotel and cruise guests
item.description: The article explores the increasingly popular practice of towel art in hotels and cruise ships, where cabin stewards transform ordinary towels into decorative shapes such as swans, frogs, dogs, crocod...

item.title: Letters to the editor: Perspectives on Alberta separatism debate, national unity, energy policy and Canadian municipal politics
item.description: This collection of letters to the editor reflects a range of Canadian public opinion on current political, energy, and cultural issues. One contributor comments on Alberta Premier Danielle Smith’s app...

item.title: Questions raised over parliamentary oversight of the Department of Parliamentary Services
item.description: The article examines the limited public and parliamentary scrutiny applied to the Department of Parliamentary Services (DPS), the body responsible for providing operational, administrative and support...

item.title: IPL rules clarify eligibility of late injury replacements for retention ahead of auctions
item.description: The Indian Premier League (IPL) has clarified an important rule regarding injury replacements and their eligibility for retention ahead of future auctions. According to the IPL’s regulations, if a pla...

item.title: A Retrospective on Rose Valois and the Golden Age of Parisian Hat Design
item.description: The article explores the legacy of Rose Valois, one of the most influential French millinery houses of the mid-20th century. Founded in Paris in 1927 by Fernande Léger, Germaine de Valois, and Rose Ma...

item.title: Mark Seliger’s 1999 Rolling Stone Portraits Presented Brad Pitt in a Surreal and Unconventional Style
item.description: The article revisits a memorable 1999 photo session in which photographer Mark Seliger captured actor Brad Pitt for Rolling Stone magazine. The portraits stand out because they departed from the polis...

item.title: Australian activists freed after Gaza aid flotilla detention allege mistreatment in Israeli custody
item.description: Several Australians who took part in a Gaza-bound aid flotilla have returned home after being detained by Israeli authorities in international waters west of Cyprus. Eleven Australians were among arou...

item.title: How Krack Cream Built a Market Leader by Solving the Overlooked Problem of Cracked Heels
item.description: This article presents a business case study on Krack Cream, a skincare product developed by Paras Pharmaceuticals that successfully identified and addressed an overlooked yet widespread problem: crack...
